background: We select an SRS of n=712 from a population of 20-29-year-old men and calculate x̄=183 pounds. The population standard deviation (σ) is 40.

task.query: We propose to calculate 90% confidence intervals for μ based on this information.
We propose to calculate 95% confidence intervals for μ based on this information.
We propose to calculate 99% confidence intervals for μ based on this information.

meta info.caption: Confidence intervals, various levels of confidence (Body weight, 20-29-year-old U.S. males)
meta info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\mu$ When $\sigma$ Is Known
